_description: Panel-level script. Takes the scene script beats and assigns them to specific panels within pages. Adds lettering details, acting direction, balloon styling, and reading flow. This is the visual storytelling / editing layer.

pages.page_turn_context: Opening page — establishes Adèle alone in her world before the disruption

captions.text: Tuesday. 9:47 PM. The toaster had been broken since March.

CHARACTER_A.expression: Brow furrowed in concentration, lips slightly pursed
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Hunched forward over the table, both hands gripping the butter knife
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Down — locked onto the screw head
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Thumb adjusting knife angle against the Phillips screw

panels.reader_focus: Adèle's hands on the toaster — the intimacy of the task

captions.text: Probably a Phillips head. Most likely a number two. The butter knife was... statistically adequate.

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Leaning in closer, knife slipping on metal
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Down — studying the screw resistance
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Knife scraping, leaving a thin scratch on chrome

panels.reader_focus: The scratch on the toaster — first sign of failure

CHARACTER_A.expression: Glasses pushed up with wrist, flat stare at the toaster
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Sitting back slightly, arms resting on table edge
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Level — regarding the toaster like an adversary
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Pushing glasses up with the back of her wrist

panels.reader_focus: The empty apartment around her — the quiet loneliness of the space

pages.page_rhythm_note: Three horizontal panels, each progressively wider in emotional scope. Start tight on hands, end wide on the apartment silence.

pages.page_turn_context: Page turn reveals the disruption — the knock should feel like a physical jolt

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Shoulders jerked up, butter knife mid-clatter onto table
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Snapping toward the door
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Dropping the butter knife reflexively

panels.reader_focus: The violence of the knock — breaking the established quiet

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Standing at the open door, one hand on the handle
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the flamingo, then slowly up to Thomas
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Grip tightening on the door handle

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Leaning against the doorframe, flamingo tucked under one arm like a baguette
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Slight lean forward, casual as borrowing sugar

panels.reader_focus: The flamingo — the absurd centerpiece revealed

speech_balloons.text: Hey. So — quick thing. Completely unrelated to anything. Can I use your bathtub?

CHARACTER_B.expression: Eyebrows raised innocently, hint of a smirk
CHARACTER_B.body_language: Gesturing vaguely toward the apartment interior
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Slight head tilt, selling the casual pitch

panels.reader_focus: Thomas's delivery — the bathtub question landing

speech_balloons.text: Thomas... what is that.
It's a flamingo. Obviously. The colour is — look, that's a whole separate conversation.

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Arms crossed, blocking the doorway reflexively
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Down at the flamingo, disbelief
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: One hand gesturing limply at the bird

CHARACTER_B.expression: Waving off the colour question, deflecting
CHARACTER_B.body_language: Already half-stepping past her into the apartment
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: Looking past Adèle toward the hallway
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Shifting the flamingo to his other arm

panels.reader_focus: The exchange — Adèle's deadpan vs Thomas's deflection

pages.page_rhythm_note: Impact → Reveal → Punchline → Volley. The page reads as a comedic escalation from silence to absurdity.

pages.page_turn_context: The negotiation — Thomas steamrolling Adèle's resistance

speech_balloons.text: My plumbing is — you don't want details. Trust me. The point is, she needs water. Shallow water. Your tub is perfect.
She.

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Gesturing at the bathtub like a real estate agent showing a penthouse
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: Sweeping between Adèle and the tub
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Pointing emphatically at the empty porcelain

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Standing in the doorway, arms at sides, not entering
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At Thomas, processing the pronoun
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Slight backward lean — recoiling from the implication

panels.reader_focus: Thomas's sales pitch crashing into Adèle's one-word response

captions.text: It's 10 PM on a Tuesday. There is a green bird in my hallway. This is my life now.

speech_balloons.text: Her name is Giselle. Which — I didn't pick. She came pre-named.

CHARACTER_B.expression: Defensive pride, like introducing a girlfriend
CHARACTER_B.body_language: One hand on the flamingo's back, protective
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Slight puff of the chest

CHARACTER_A.expression: Eyes closed, pinching the bridge of her nose
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Weight shifting, resigned lean against the doorframe
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Eyes shut — processing
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Slow exhale through the nose

panels.reader_focus: Adèle's internal monologue — the moment she gives up fighting

speech_balloons.text: ...bathroom's on the left.

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Already turning away toward the kitchen, waving vaguely at the bathroom
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Away — toward her toaster refuge
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Limp wave in the direction of the bathroom

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Marching past her with the flamingo, victorious
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Quick wink at Adèle as he passes

panels.reader_focus: The surrender — Adèle walking away, Thomas marching in

pages.page_rhythm_note: Three panels: pitch → breaking point → surrender. The asymmetric layout gives the middle panel (Adèle's internal monologue) the most space.

pages.page_turn_context: Time skip — the clicking sound draws Adèle back

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Paused mid-task at the kitchen table, butter knife hovering
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Toward the bathroom door — off-panel
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Ear slightly turned toward the sound

panels.reader_focus: The sound — tiny, rhythmic, out of place

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Standing in the bathroom doorway, leaning on the frame
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Down at Thomas and the flamingo
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Hand gripping the doorframe

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Sitting on the tub edge, palm open with corn kernels
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: At the flamingo's beak, watching it eat
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Fingers gently releasing one kernel at a time

panels.reader_focus: The reveal — Thomas feeding the bird like it's the most natural thing in the world

speech_balloons.text: You brought... food for it.
Her. And yeah, she likes the dry kind better. The wet stuff makes her — anyway. It's a texture thing.

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Arms folded loosely, weight on one hip
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the bag of corn on the floor
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Chin dropping slightly

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Shrugging mid-sentence, casual deflection
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: At the flamingo, avoiding Adèle's eyes
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Interrupting himself with a hand wave

panels.reader_focus: The corn as evidence — Thomas planned this

CHARACTER_A.expression: Softening — a crack in the analytical armor
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Leaning slightly forward, drawn in despite herself
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Meeting the flamingo's eye
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Unconscious half-step into the bathroom

panels.reader_focus: The flamingo looking at Adèle — a connection forming

pages.page_rhythm_note: Sound → Reveal → Dialogue → Silent connection. Four panels building from suspicion to curiosity.

pages.page_turn_context: The bird is alive — the central shock of the story

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Body frozen, one hand suspended mid-air
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Locked on the flamingo's blinking eye
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Breath caught — visible stillness

panels.reader_focus: The blink — the moment the bird becomes real

speech_balloons.text: Thomas. That's... that is a real bird.
Bankrupt roadside zoo. Outside Valence. She was the last one left. They were just... going to leave her.

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Hand reaching partway toward the bird, then stopping
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Between the bird and Thomas
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Swallowing — processing

CHARACTER_B.expression: Serious for once — no mask, no grin
CHARACTER_B.body_language: Hands resting on his knees, shoulders dropped
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: At the flamingo, not at Adèle
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Voice dropping — no performance

panels.reader_focus: Thomas's rare sincerity — the truth behind the stunt

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Standing still, arms at sides
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the flamingo shifting its weight
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Faint nod — unconscious acceptance

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Still seated on tub edge, hands clasped
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Faint smile starting at the corners

panels.reader_focus: The shared silence — something real has entered the room

pages.page_rhythm_note: Three horizontal panels: shock → truth → silence. The center panel carries the emotional weight.

pages.page_turn_context: The emotional release — Adèle laughing on the floor

speech_balloons.text: Okay, one thing. Mrs. Gable absolutely cannot find out about this.
Thomas. It's four feet tall.
I told her it was a large parrot.

CHARACTER_B.expression: Dead serious — finger raised, conspiratorial lean
CHARACTER_B.body_language: Leaning forward from the tub edge, one finger raised like giving a briefing
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Index finger wagging

CHARACTER_A.expression: Deadpan disbelief cracking into the first tremor of laughter
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Starting to slide down the wall, knees giving way
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At Thomas, then ceiling — giving up
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Hand covering her mouth, shoulders starting to shake

panels.reader_focus: The 'large parrot' line as the comedic climax of the chapter

captions.text: She hadn't laughed like that since before Marc.

CHARACTER_A.expression: Head tilted back, genuine laughter, tears at the corners
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Sitting on the bathmat, back against the tiled wall, shoulders shaking
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Up at the ceiling, eyes crinkling
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: One hand on the floor for balance, the other over her stomach

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Sitting back on the tub edge, relaxed posture
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: At Adèle — taking in the moment
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Quiet smile — no need to perform anymore

panels.reader_focus: Adèle on the floor — the release. The narration grounds the emotion.

pages.page_rhythm_note: Splash-style layout: the top panel is the comedic exchange, the bottom is the wide emotional payoff. High angle looking down at Adèle on the bathmat.

pages.page_turn_context: The bonding deepens — cold pizza and childhood echoes

CHARACTER_A.expression: Relaxed, small smile, guard fully down
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Sitting cross-legged on the floor, holding a pizza slice
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the pizza box between them
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Picking at a piece of cheese

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Legs stretched out, leaning back against the tub
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Eating the crust first

panels.reader_focus: The overhead tableau — two siblings, pizza, a flamingo in a bathtub

speech_balloons.text: This is exactly like Lyon. Floor pizza. Terrible lighting. Except the bird is new.
The lighting in Lyon was worse. You had that... that lamp shaped like a football.

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Waving a pizza slice for emphasis
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Gesturing around the bathroom as if it's the old room

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Shoulders softening, leaning into the memory
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Into middle distance — remembering
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Pizza slice forgotten in hand

panels.reader_focus: The Lyon callback — the past overlaying the present

captions.text: Same positions. Same cold pizza. He still eats the crust first.

speech_balloons.text: That lamp was a design classic.

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Leaning forward, jabbing the crust at her
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Crust-pointing like a sword

panels.reader_focus: Adèle's internal observation — the tender detail of crust-first eating

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Relaxed against the wall, pizza box between them
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the flamingo standing on one leg
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Slow blink — content

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Head resting against the tub rim
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Chewing the last of his crust

panels.reader_focus: The silence — warmth without words. The radiator hisses.

pages.page_rhythm_note: Wide establishing overhead → dialogue → tender detail → held silence. The page should feel warm and unhurried.

pages.page_turn_context: Two memory scenes sharing a page — the haircut and the pennies

speech_balloons.text: Remember the kitchen shears? I wore a beanie for a MONTH because of you.
You moved. I told you not to move. Statistically, one hundred percent your fault.

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Pointing at Adèle's face, leaning forward
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Jabbing his finger for emphasis

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Arms folded, chin slightly raised
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: Level at Thomas — deadpan
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: One eyebrow micro-raise

panels.reader_focus: The ping-pong of accusation and defense

speech_balloons.text: The beanie was bright red. I looked like a — like a postbox with legs.
...you lost the red one in two weeks.

CHARACTER_B.expression: Laughing at his own joke, tugging the green beanie
CHARACTER_B.body_language: One hand pulling at his current beanie, self-aware
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: Down at his own beanie, then at Adèle
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Tugging the green beanie — visual callback

CHARACTER_A.expression: Quiet knowing smile, looking at the green beanie
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Leaning back, arms relaxed
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the green beanie — the pattern
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Trailing off mid-sentence

panels.reader_focus: The beanie callback — green replaces red, habit endures

speech_balloons.text: And the sofa. On the stairs. Nobody could get past for three days.
Madame Fournier had to use the service elevator. She called us ghost children.

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Gesturing with both hands, storyteller mode
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Hands painting the scene in the air

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Leaning forward, engaged
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At Thomas, matching his energy
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Laughing with her whole face

panels.reader_focus: The shared story — both contributing, building together

captions.text: He bought candy with those pennies. Split it exactly in half. Even the broken pieces.

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Open palm extended, three copper pennies catching the light
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: Down at the pennies in his hand
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Thumb gently turning one penny over

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Leaning toward his open palm
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the pennies — the past made physical
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Breath held

panels.reader_focus: The pennies — a physical artifact of their childhood bond

pages.page_rhythm_note: Two scenes compressed into one page — fast memories (haircut) transitioning into a tender moment (pennies). The final panel should be the largest, holding the silence.

pages.page_turn_context: Playful chaos — the flamingo learns to beep

speech_balloons.text: Okay okay okay. Watch this. Ready?

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Crouching next to the microwave, one hand holding a treat toward the bird
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Mouth shaped for the beep sound

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Leaning against the kitchen counter, watching
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At Thomas, eyebrow raised
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Slight head shake

panels.reader_focus: Thomas's setup — the anticipation

speech_balloons.text: That's... that is statistically improbable.

CHARACTER_A.expression: Astonished delight — mouth open, adjusting glasses
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Pushing off the counter, leaning forward
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the flamingo, wide-eyed
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Adjusting glasses — nervous habit when surprised

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Standing up, arms raised
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: At the flamingo, proud parent energy
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Fist clenching in celebration

panels.reader_focus: The flamingo's beep — the impossible made real

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Doubling over, trying to contain laughter
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the wall — the barking is coming from there
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Pressing a hand against the wall separating apartments

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Hands up in surrender, backing away from the bird
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: At the wall, then at Adèle
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Shushing gesture at the flamingo

panels.reader_focus: The chaos — flamingo beeping, dog barking, siblings laughing

speech_balloons.text: Napoleon.

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Frozen, one finger raised
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Mouthing the name like a curse

panels.reader_focus: The one-word reaction — Thomas realizing the consequences

pages.page_rhythm_note: Build → Payoff → Chaos → Punchline. The page escalates from anticipation to pandemonium, then cuts to Thomas's single-word regret.

pages.page_turn_context: The farewell — Thomas departs, the silence returns

speech_balloons.text: Right. Early start tomorrow. Big plans. Very legitimate plans.

CHARACTER_B.body_language: Scooping flamingo under one arm, pizza box in the other, moving toward the door
CHARACTER_B.gaze_direction: Toward the door — forward, not back
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Tucking the flamingo under his arm with practiced ease

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Standing in the living room, arms at sides
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At Thomas's back
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Fingers curling, almost reaching out

panels.reader_focus: Thomas's transparent deflection — 'legitimate plans' fools no one

speech_balloons.text: Thomas...

CHARACTER_B.expression: Sincere smile — no joke, no deflection
CHARACTER_B.body_language: Paused in the open doorway, backlit by hallway light, flamingo silhouette beside him
CHARACTER_B.micro_action: Small nod — acknowledgment without words

CHARACTER_A.expression: Words trailing off, emotions she can't name
CHARACTER_A.body_language: One step forward, hand half-raised
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At Thomas in the doorway light
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Mouth opening, then closing — the sentence dies

panels.reader_focus: The silhouette in the doorway — the unfinished sentence

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Standing alone in the living room, door closed
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the closed door
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Light fading from under the door as Thomas walks away

panels.reader_focus: The empty apartment — the silence that fills the vacuum Thomas leaves

pages.page_rhythm_note: Three horizontal panels: departure → doorway moment → silence. The final panel should feel like a held breath.

pages.page_turn_context: Final page — the corn replaces the toaster. Acceptance.

CHARACTER_A.body_language: Standing at the kitchen counter, not looking at the toaster. Reaching into a grocery bag.
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the bag of corn in her hand
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Pulling the corn bag from the grocery bag — deliberate

panels.reader_focus: Adèle choosing the corn over the toaster — the pivot

CHARACTER_A.expression: A smile she doesn't know she's making
CHARACTER_A.body_language: Placing the corn bag on the middle shelf, next to colour-coded spice jars. Adjusting it so it sits straight.
CHARACTER_A.gaze_direction: At the corn bag on the shelf — satisfied
CHARACTER_A.micro_action: Nudging the bag into alignment with the spice jars — her orderliness embracing chaos

panels.reader_focus: The corn nestled among the spice jars — order making room for disorder. The final image.

pages.page_rhythm_note: Splash page split into two visual moments: the choice (picking up the corn) and the act (placing it in the cabinet). The final narration closes the story.
